events.text: Hi Sage! I have one test case from my test list I need to run: it is named "Weekday pickup hours are shown". I just want to run that one test and confirm it passes — not the whole set. Where do I start?

That's exactly what this space is for — and it's all practice, so nothing can break. Here's where to start: Get your bearings. Open Code Studio from the desktop (double-click its icon) and read README.md. Then open tests/pickup.spec.js and find the test named "Weekday pickup hours are shown". Want to see the page like a visitor? Open Garden Site too. Don't change anything.

npm test -- "Weekday pickup hours are shown"

events.text: Honest truth: my first npm test ran all three and I almost called it done — it was reading that it said 1 test and named my exact title that finally made me trust the result was really mine. Thank you for the nudge to slow down!
That's the whole loop — and it's verified, so take the credit. If you want, try it once more your own way; otherwise you're all set here.
